Acting roles in German film and television

profiles.name: Maximilian Mundt
profiles.tagline: Lead of Netflix's How to Sell Drugs Online Fast — Germany's biggest streaming star with Russian paternal roots
profiles.bio: Maximilian Mundt is a German actor who became internationally known as the lead of How to Sell Drugs Online Fast (Netflix) — one of the most popular German-language series on Netflix globally. His father has Russian roots.
profiles.connectionNote: Mundt's father has Russian heritage, connecting him to the Russian-German diaspora. His international breakthrough through Netflix's German-language series represents the new generation of German entertainment talent reaching global audiences through streaming platforms.
achievements: Lead role in How to Sell Drugs Online Fast (Netflix, 2019-2021) — international hit
One of Germany's most internationally recognised young actors
German acting awards recognition
Netflix series watched in 190 countries
Established career in German film and television

profiles.name: Marina Weisband
profiles.tagline: Kyiv-born political director of Germany's Pirate Party — a new kind of democratic voice
profiles.bio: Marina Weisband is a Ukrainian-born German politician and psychologist who served as political director of the German Pirate Party during its breakthrough period (2011-2012) and became one of Germany's most thoughtful public voices on digital rights, democracy, and education. Born in Kyiv, she emigrated to Germany as a teenager.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Kyiv in 1987 and emigrating to Germany with her Jewish family in the 1990s, Weisband is part of the large Ukrainian-Jewish diaspora that transformed Germany's intellectual and political landscape. Her political career — advocating for digital rights, transparency, and participatory democracy — reflects the values of someone who experienced the Soviet system firsthand.
achievements: Political Director of the German Pirate Party (2011-2012) during its electoral breakthrough
One of Germany's most widely respected voices on digital democracy
Regular commentator in major German media (ARD, ZDF, Spiegel)
Author and educator on digital rights and democratic participation
Spoke publicly against Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine

profiles.name: Sergey Lagodinsky
profiles.tagline: Volgograd-born MEP who became Germany's leading Russian-origin voice in the European Parliament
profiles.bio: Sergey Lagodinsky is a Russian-born German politician of the Green Party who has served as a Member of the European Parliament since 2019. Born in Volgograd (then Stalingrad) into a Jewish family, he emigrated to Germany and became one of the most articulate voices in the EU Parliament on Russia policy, democracy, and minority rights.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Volgograd in 1975 and emigrating to Germany as part of the Jewish emigration wave of the 1990s, Lagodinsky's entire career has been shaped by his experience of the Soviet Jewish world and his commitment to European democratic values. His parliamentary work on Russia sanctions and rule of law is directly informed by his origins.
achievements: Member of the European Parliament (2019-present) — Green Party
Co-rapporteur on multiple EU-Russia and democracy-related legislation
One of the most prominent Russian-origin politicians in the EU institutions
Author and commentator on EU-Russia relations
Spokesperson on justice and rule of law issues

profiles.name: Paul Ziemiak
profiles.tagline: CDU politician and former party secretary-general with Eastern European roots
profiles.bio: Paul Ziemiak is a German CDU politician born in Stettin (now Szczecin, Poland) who served as Secretary-General of the CDU (2018-2021) — one of the most powerful positions in German centre-right politics. He is one of a generation of German politicians with Eastern European immigrant backgrounds reshaping German conservatism.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Stettin (then part of Poland, a region with deep historical connections to both the Russian Empire and German culture) in 1985 and emigrating to Germany as a child, Ziemiak's family background connects him to the broader Eastern European diaspora in Germany. His political career in the CDU represents the integration of Eastern European immigrant communities into German mainstream politics.
achievements: Secretary-General of the CDU (2018-2021)
Member of the Bundestag (2013-present)
Chairman of the Junge Union (CDU youth wing, 2014-2019)
One of the most prominent politicians with Eastern European immigrant background in German conservatism
Key figure in CDU's post-Merkel transformation

profiles.name: Walter Homolka
profiles.tagline: Germany's leading liberal rabbi — rebuilt Jewish life in Germany with Russian-Jewish diaspora roots
profiles.bio: Rabbi Professor Walter Homolka is one of Germany's most prominent Jewish religious figures — the founding rector of the Abraham Geiger College, Germany's first rabbinical seminary since the Holocaust. He has been a central figure in rebuilding liberal Jewish life in Germany, drawing heavily on the Russian-Jewish diaspora that transformed German Jewish communities after 1990.
profiles.connectionNote: Homolka's family has connections to the Eastern European Jewish world, and his entire career has been devoted to serving and rebuilding the German Jewish community that is now predominantly Russian-speaking in its origin. The Abraham Geiger College he founded trains rabbis for communities whose members are overwhelmingly from the former Soviet Union.
achievements: Founding Rector of Abraham Geiger College — Germany's first post-Holocaust rabbinical seminary
Professor of Modern Jewish Thought at University of Potsdam
Central figure in rebuilding liberal Judaism in Germany
Author of multiple books on Jewish theology and liberal Judaism
Serves communities predominantly composed of Russian-Jewish immigrants

profiles.name: Katharina Thalbach
profiles.tagline: Berlin theatrical legend with Russian theatrical roots — the most beloved actress on the German-speaking stage
profiles.bio: Katharina Thalbach is a German actress and director who became one of the most celebrated performers on the German-speaking stage and screen, winning multiple German Film Awards and the Bavarian Film Award. Known for her theatrical intensity and her work with Bertolt Brecht's legacy in Berlin, she is a national theatrical treasure.
profiles.connectionNote: Thalbach's family has deep connections to the Russian theatrical tradition through the Berliner Ensemble and its roots in Russian theatrical theory. Her mother Sabine Thalbach and her artistic lineage connect directly to the Russian-influenced Brechtian theatre that shaped East German cultural life. She grew up steeped in this Russian-German theatrical fusion.
achievements: Multiple German Film Award (Lola) wins
Bavarian Film Award
Principal actress at the Berliner Ensemble and major German theatres
Directed and acted in Brecht productions — keeper of the tradition
One of the most decorated actresses in German theatrical history

profiles.name: Mark Waschke
profiles.tagline: Star of Dark (Netflix) — Germany's most internationally recognised dramatic actor with Russian family roots
profiles.bio: Mark Waschke is a German actor best known internationally for his role as Noah/Adam in Netflix's Dark (2017-2020) — the acclaimed German-language science fiction series that became one of Netflix's most-watched non-English productions globally. He has Russian family roots on one side.
profiles.connectionNote: Waschke has Russian ancestry through one family branch, connecting him to the Russian-German story that runs through so much of German cultural life. His international breakthrough through Dark — playing a character who travels across centuries — brought German-language drama to a genuinely global audience.
achievements: Dark (Netflix, 2017-2020) — Noah/Adam across 3 seasons, global audience of millions
One of Germany's most internationally recognised actors through Dark
German theatrical career spanning major stages
Tatort (German crime series) — recurring role
Breakthrough to international audiences through Netflix

profiles.name: Shirin David
profiles.tagline: Germany's biggest female rapper with Lithuanian-Russian Jewish roots — 4 million YouTube subscribers
profiles.bio: Shirin David (born Barbara Shirin Davidavičius) is a German rapper, singer, and content creator who became Germany's most successful female rapper and one of the country's most-followed YouTube personalities. Her family name reveals her Lithuanian-Jewish origins.
profiles.connectionNote: Her family name Davidavičius is distinctly Lithuanian Jewish — her mother has Lithuanian and Russian Jewish ancestry, connecting her to the Russian Empire's Baltic Jewish communities. Growing up in Hamburg with this heritage, her stage persona Shirin David merges German hip-hop culture with an immigrant family background.
achievements: Germany's most successful female rapper
4+ million YouTube subscribers
Multiple German chart No. 1 albums including Supersize (2022)
Collaborations with major German and international artists
Over 1 billion streams across platforms

profiles.name: Bernd Leno
profiles.tagline: German national team goalkeeper of Russian-German Aussiedler descent — Arsenal and Fulham Premier League star
profiles.bio: Bernd Leno is a German professional goalkeeper who played for Bayer Leverkusen, Arsenal FC, and Fulham FC in the Premier League, and represented Germany's national team. His family are Russlanddeutsche — ethnic Germans from Russia who emigrated to Germany.
profiles.connectionNote: Leno's family are Aussiedler — ethnic Germans from Russia who resettled in Germany. His family's Russian-German background places him in the community of millions of Russians-Germans who transformed German society after 1990, and whose children have risen to the highest levels of German sport, politics, and culture.
achievements: Arsenal FC goalkeeper (2018-2022) — Premier League
Fulham FC (2022-present) — Premier League
Bayer Leverkusen — DFB-Pokal winner
German national team — 10+ caps
One of Germany's most established Premier League goalkeepers

profiles.name: Waldemar Anton
profiles.tagline: Born in Russia, became a Bundesliga star for Hannover and Stuttgart — German international
profiles.bio: Waldemar Anton is a Russian-born German professional footballer who plays as a centre-back for Borussia Dortmund and the German national team. Born in Russia, he grew up in Germany and represented Germany at youth level before breaking into the Bundesliga.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Russia in 1996 and emigrating to Germany as a child, Anton is part of the generation of Russian-German players who have enriched the Bundesliga. His career — from Hannover 96 to Stuttgart to Dortmund and the German national team — is a product of both Russian origins and German football development.
achievements: VfB Stuttgart — Bundesliga regular
Borussia Dortmund (2024-present)
German national team — international caps
Hannover 96 — key player in Bundesliga promotion
Born in Russia, raised and developed through German football

profiles.name: Slava Bykov
profiles.tagline: Chelyabinsk-born world champion who became Switzerland's greatest ice hockey coach
profiles.bio: Vyacheslav (Slava) Bykov is a Russian-Swiss ice hockey player and coach who had a brilliant playing career winning multiple World Championships and Olympic medals before becoming one of the most successful coaches in European hockey — leading Russia to back-to-back World Championship gold medals (2008, 2009) and coaching in Switzerland.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Chelyabinsk in 1960 — one of Russia's great hockey cities — Bykov played for CSKA Moscow and the Soviet national team before emigrating to Switzerland where he played and later coached. His career bridges the Soviet hockey tradition and Swiss sporting culture.
achievements: World Ice Hockey Champion as player — multiple times
Olympic Silver and Bronze Medal as player
Head Coach of Russian national team — World Championship gold (2008, 2009)
Coached Swiss national team and Swiss clubs
One of the most decorated figures in European hockey

profiles.name: Viktorija Golubic
profiles.tagline: Swiss WTA professional with Yugoslav-Russian roots — Zurich-born and Swiss national team stalwart
profiles.bio: Viktorija Golubic is a Swiss professional tennis player born in Zurich who has been one of Switzerland's most consistent WTA Tour competitors and a stalwart of the Swiss Fed Cup / Billie Jean King Cup team alongside Belinda Bencic.
profiles.connectionNote: Golubic's family has Yugoslav and Russian roots — her background connects her to the Slavic diaspora in Switzerland. Her surname Golubic is distinctly South Slavic. She has been a key contributor to Swiss tennis success alongside the more famous Bencic and Bacsinszky.
achievements: WTA Tour professional — consistent top-100 ranking
Swiss Billie Jean King Cup team member — multiple seasons
WTA titles on the tour
One of Switzerland's most capped Fed Cup players
Career-high ranking approaching top 50

profiles.name: Oskar Hartmann
profiles.tagline: Russian-born serial entrepreneur who co-founded Westwing and KK&K — Germany's startup ecosystem connector
profiles.bio: Oskar Hartmann is a Russian-born German entrepreneur who co-founded Westwing (European home decor e-commerce), KK&K, and other ventures, becoming one of the most connected figures in Germany's startup ecosystem. He is also known for his personal development content and public speaking.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Russia and emigrating to Germany, Hartmann built his entrepreneurial career in the German startup ecosystem. His Russian background and German business career make him a representative figure of the post-Soviet entrepreneurial generation that found its commercial home in Western Europe.
achievements: Co-founded Westwing — European home decor e-commerce platform
Co-founded KK&K — investment and consulting
Active angel investor in German startup ecosystem
Popular speaker and author on entrepreneurship and personal development
One of Germany's most connected Russian-born entrepreneurs

profiles.name: Nikita Zhivotovskiy
profiles.tagline: Russian-born mathematics professor at TU Munich — one of Europe's leading combinatorists
profiles.bio: Nikita Zhivotovskiy is a Russian-born mathematician and professor at the Technical University of Munich (TUM) who is a leading researcher in combinatorics, probability theory, and statistical learning theory. He has received significant recognition for his contributions to mathematical foundations of machine learning.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Russia and educated in the Russian mathematical tradition before moving to Germany, Zhivotovskiy represents the continued flow of Russian mathematical talent to Western European institutions. His work at TUM on the mathematical foundations of machine learning bridges Russia's strong combinatorics tradition with cutting-edge applied mathematics.
achievements: Professor at Technical University of Munich (TUM)
Leading researcher in combinatorics and statistical learning theory
Significant contributions to mathematical foundations of machine learning
ERC (European Research Council) grant recipient
Part of TUM's world-class mathematics faculty

profiles.name: Mikhail Lukin
profiles.tagline: Moscow-born Harvard physics professor — one of the world's leading quantum computing scientists
profiles.bio: Mikhail Lukin is a Russian-American physicist and professor at Harvard University who is one of the world's foremost experts in quantum optics and quantum information science. Co-director of the Harvard-MIT Center for Ultracold Atoms, his work on quantum computing and quantum networks is among the most cited in physics.
profiles.connectionNote: Born and educated in Russia before emigrating to the United States, Lukin is a product of the Soviet physics tradition that produced so many world-class scientists. At Harvard, he has become one of the most influential figures in the rapidly advancing field of quantum computing — a field where Russian-trained scientists are disproportionately represented.
achievements: Professor of Physics, Harvard University
Co-Director, Harvard-MIT Center for Ultracold Atoms
One of the most highly cited physicists in quantum information science
Willis E. Lamb Award for Laser Science and Quantum Optics
Pioneer in quantum computing, quantum networks, and quantum sensing

profiles.name: Kostja Ullmann
profiles.tagline: Russian-born German actor known for Sommersturm and major German romantic comedies
profiles.bio: Kostja Ullmann is a Russian-born German actor who became known through the coming-of-age film Sommersturm (2004) and has built a career in German film and television. Born in Russia and emigrating to Germany, he is one of the more prominent Russian-born actors in mainstream German entertainment.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Russia and growing up in Germany, Ullmann's career in German mainstream cinema and television represents the integration of Russian-born talent into German popular culture. His Russian origins and German career make him a representative figure of the Aussiedler and Russian Jewish generation that reshaped German cultural life.
achievements: Sommersturm (2004) — breakthrough German film
Multiple German romantic comedies and mainstream productions
German television career spanning two decades
Russian-born actor in German mainstream entertainment
Part of Germany's Russian-origin acting community

profiles.name: Olexesh
profiles.tagline: Ukrainian-born German rapper with millions of streams — one of Germany's authentic Russian-speaking hip-hop voices
profiles.bio: Olexesh (born Olexij Kossarev) is a Ukrainian-born German rapper who became one of Germany's more distinctive hip-hop voices, blending streetwise German rap with his Ukrainian-Russian cultural background. His albums have charted in Germany and attracted millions of streams.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Ukraine (USSR) and emigrating to Germany, Olexesh brings an authentic post-Soviet perspective to German hip-hop — his lyrics and persona reflect the experience of growing up between Ukrainian-Russian and German cultures. He is one of several Ukrainian/Russian-born rappers who have shaped the sound of German street rap.
achievements: Multiple German chart appearances
Millions of streams across platforms
Authentic Ukrainian-Russian voice in German hip-hop
Several studio albums released in Germany
Part of Germany's diverse rap scene with post-Soviet roots

profiles.name: Anatoly Kuznetsov
profiles.tagline: Born in Kyiv — survived Babi Yar as a boy, wrote the first eyewitness account of the massacre
profiles.bio: Anatoly Kuznetsov was a Soviet-British author born in Kyiv who survived the German occupation of Kyiv as a teenager — including the mass executions at Babi Yar. His documentary novel Babi Yar (1966), published in censored form in the USSR and then in full after his defection to Britain in 1969, provided the world with its first definitive eyewitness account of the massacre of 33,771 Jews in two days.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Kyiv in 1929, Kuznetsov witnessed the German occupation as a child and saw the Babi Yar ravine turned into a mass grave. His novel — written with documentary photographs, testimonies, and his own witness — broke the Soviet silence on Babi Yar and became one of the most important Holocaust documents ever written. He defected to Britain in 1969 during a trip to London.
achievements: Babi Yar (1966) — first major literary account of the Babi Yar massacre
Defected to Britain (1969) — published the full uncensored text
One of the most important Holocaust documentary novels ever written
Witnessed Babi Yar as a 12-year-old boy — eyewitness testimony of 33,771 murdered
BBC Russian Service broadcaster after defection

profiles.name: Aharon Appelfeld
profiles.tagline: Escaped a Nazi camp at age 9, hid in Ukrainian forests for years — became Israel's greatest Holocaust author
profiles.bio: Aharon Appelfeld was an Israeli novelist born in Jadova (then Romania, now Ukraine) whose mother was murdered at the start of the German invasion and who was deported to a concentration camp as a child. He escaped and survived for years hiding in the Ukrainian forests before making his way to Israel, where he became one of the most celebrated Hebrew-language authors of the 20th century.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in 1932 in a region that shifted between Romania and Soviet-occupied Ukraine, Appelfeld grew up in the borderland world of the Russian Empire's Jewish communities before the Holocaust destroyed it. His novels — Badenheim 1939, The Age of Wonders, The Iron Tracks — reconstruct the world of Central and Eastern European Jewish life before its annihilation with extraordinary delicacy.
achievements: Over 40 novels and story collections — translated into 30+ languages
Israel Prize for Literature (1983) — Israel's highest honour
Man Booker International Prize shortlist — multiple times
Badenheim 1939 (1978) — most celebrated novel
Honorary doctorates from Hebrew University and multiple international universities
profiles.quote: I write about the world that was destroyed. I am its last witness.

profiles.name: Felix Zandman
profiles.tagline: Survived the Holocaust hidden in a pit for 17 months — then built Vishay Intertechnology, one of the world's largest electronics companies
profiles.bio: Felix Zandman was a Polish-American physicist and entrepreneur born in Grodno (then Russian Empire, now Belarus) who survived the Holocaust by hiding in a cramped underground dugout for 17 months alongside six other people. He emerged, studied physics in Paris, and founded Vishay Intertechnology — one of the world's largest manufacturers of electronic components, used in virtually every electronic device on earth.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Grodno (a city that passed from the Russian Empire to Poland to Soviet occupation) in 1927, Zandman lost most of his family in the Holocaust. His survival in a pit — barely large enough to stand in, in temperatures of both extreme heat and cold — is one of the most extraordinary Holocaust survival stories. His transformation from that pit to building a global electronics empire embodies the immigrant will to rebuild.
achievements: Founded Vishay Intertechnology — one of the world's largest electronic components manufacturers
Survived 17 months hidden in an underground dugout during the Holocaust
Developed foil resistors — foundational to modern electronics
Israel Prize for Industry and Innovation
Never Too Young to Remember (1999) — acclaimed Holocaust memoir
profiles.quote: I survived to build, not just to remember.

profiles.name: Abakar Saidov
profiles.tagline: Dagestani-born AI entrepreneur who co-founded Beamery — a $800M+ AI talent management platform
profiles.bio: Abakar Saidov is a British-Dagestani entrepreneur who co-founded Beamery alongside Sultan Saidov — an AI-powered talent management platform valued at over $800 million. Born in Dagestan, Russia, he spent his early childhood in the USSR/CIS before his parents — a theoretical physicist father and neurologist mother — emigrated to Britain in the mid-1990s.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Dagestan (Russia) and raised in the post-Soviet CIS before emigrating to the UK, Saidov carries the intellectual tradition of a family of Soviet scientists into the world of AI entrepreneurship. His Dagestani roots — the mountainous North Caucasian republic with a deeply distinct culture within the Russian sphere — give him one of the most culturally specific post-Soviet identities in British tech.

Beamery serves Fortune 500 companies globally

profiles.name: Arseniy Vershinin
profiles.tagline: Grew up in a small Siberian town, studied nuclear physics in Moscow — then co-founded Personio, Germany's $6B HR tech giant
profiles.bio: Arseniy Vershinin is a Russian-German entrepreneur who grew up in a small town in Siberia, studied at the National Research Nuclear University (MEPhI) in Moscow, then transferred to TU Munich — where he co-founded Personio, Germany's most successful HR software company, valued at $6 billion.
profiles.connectionNote: Growing up in Siberia and studying at Russia's elite nuclear physics university before building one of Germany's most valuable technology companies, Vershinin's trajectory — from provincial Siberia through Moscow's MEPhI to a $6 billion Munich startup — is one of the most striking examples of Russian scientific talent reshaping European tech.

Personio valued at $6 billion — one of Europe's most valuable HR tech companies

profiles.name: Aloona Larionova
profiles.tagline: Kyiv-based Ukrainian content creator with 440,000+ YouTube subscribers — famous as the authentic 'Slavic Girl'
profiles.bio: Aloona Larionova is a Ukrainian content creator and YouTuber based in Kyiv with over 440,000 subscribers, famous for her authentic 'Slavic Girl' persona — videos documenting Ukrainian and post-Soviet daily life, culture, and humour for an international audience. She has deep cultural and family roots in the post-Soviet world.
profiles.connectionNote: Living and streaming from Kyiv — the former capital of the Ukrainian SSR — Larionova documents post-Soviet life for an international audience with warmth and authenticity. Her content reached global audiences who discovered Soviet and Ukrainian culture through her channel during and after Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine.
achievements: 440,000+ YouTube subscribers
Famous for 'Slavic Girl' content documenting Ukrainian post-Soviet life
Continues streaming from Kyiv through Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine
International audience discovering Ukrainian/Soviet culture through her content
One of YouTube's most followed authentic Ukrainian voices

profiles.name: Ildar Shar
profiles.tagline: Russian-born Toronto entrepreneur who co-founded StackAdapt — one of Canada's most valuable adtech companies
profiles.bio: Ildar Shar is a Russian-born Canadian entrepreneur based in Toronto who co-founded StackAdapt — one of Canada's most successful advertising technology platforms — and founded FirstPrinciples, an AI research foundation. Originally from Russia and fluent in Russian, he built his career in Canada's technology ecosystem.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Russia and building his entrepreneurial career in Toronto, Shar is part of the significant Russian-born tech diaspora in Canada. His co-founding of StackAdapt — a programmatic advertising platform that has become a major player in Canadian tech — reflects the Russian engineering and entrepreneurial talent that has enriched North American technology.
achievements: Co-founded StackAdapt — one of Canada's most successful adtech platforms
Founded FirstPrinciples AI research foundation
Russian-born entrepreneur based in Toronto
Fluent Russian speaker
Active in Canadian technology and AI investment ecosystems

profiles.name: Dmitry Tokarev
profiles.tagline: Russian-born founder of Copper.co — one of the world's leading institutional crypto custodians
profiles.bio: Dmitry Tokarev is a Russian-born British entrepreneur who founded Copper.co — one of the world's leading institutional cryptocurrency custodians, safeguarding digital assets for major investment funds and financial institutions. He grew up in Russia/CIS and completed his Master's degree at Imperial College London.
profiles.connectionNote: Born and raised in Russia/CIS before studying at Imperial College London, Tokarev built Copper.co into a leading institutional crypto infrastructure company. His Russian background and his London-based career represent the post-Soviet technical talent that has made significant contributions to British fintech and the emerging digital assets industry.
achievements: Founded Copper.co — world-leading institutional crypto custodian
Copper.co safeguards digital assets for major investment funds globally
Grew up in Russia/CIS — Master's degree from Imperial College London
Built one of the most trusted names in institutional crypto infrastructure
Pioneer in regulated digital asset custody

profiles.name: Vyacheslav Kim
profiles.tagline: Co-founder of Kaspi.kz — Kazakhstan's super-app and the most valuable company in Central Asia
profiles.bio: Vyacheslav Kim is a Kazakhstani billionaire who co-founded Kaspi.kz — Kazakhstan's dominant fintech super-app combining banking, marketplace, and payments — which listed on the London Stock Exchange and NASDAQ at a valuation exceeding $18 billion. One of Kazakhstan's richest men, he conducts all international business in Russian.
profiles.connectionNote: A product of Soviet Kazakhstan and the post-Soviet entrepreneurial era, Kim built Kaspi.kz into one of the most successful fintech companies in the former Soviet space. The company's model — super-app combining bank, marketplace, and payments — has been studied as a template by fintech investors globally. All his business dealings and presentations are conducted in Russian.
achievements: Co-founded Kaspi.kz — Kazakhstan's dominant fintech super-app
Kaspi listed on LSE and NASDAQ — peak valuation $18+ billion
Kaspi serves the majority of Kazakhstan's adult population
One of Kazakhstan's wealthiest individuals (Forbes)
All international business conducted in Russian

profiles.name: Mikhail Lomtadze
profiles.tagline: Georgian-born CEO of Kaspi.kz — built Kazakhstan's most valuable company from Almaty
profiles.bio: Mikhail Lomtadze is a Georgian-born entrepreneur and CEO of Kaspi.kz — Kazakhstan's dominant fintech super-app and one of the most valuable companies in the former Soviet space. He grew up in Soviet Georgia, lives in Almaty, and runs Kaspi's global operations entirely in Russian, his primary business language.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Soviet Georgia and building his career in Kazakhstan, Lomtadze represents the pan-Soviet Russian-speaking business elite — people whose identity transcends national borders but is united by the Russian language and post-Soviet business culture. His partnership with Vyacheslav Kim built one of the former Soviet space's most successful technology companies.
achievements: CEO of Kaspi.kz — co-built Kazakhstan's dominant fintech super-app
Kaspi listed on LSE and NASDAQ — peak valuation $18+ billion
Born in Soviet Georgia — lives and works in Almaty, Kazakhstan
All business conducted in Russian — primary business language
One of the most successful post-Soviet entrepreneurs in fintech

profiles.name: Timur Kulibayev
profiles.tagline: Son-in-law of Kazakhstan's founding president — controls the country's energy and banking empires
profiles.bio: Timur Kulibayev is a Kazakhstani billionaire and the husband of Dinara Nazarbayeva — the daughter of founding President Nursultan Nazarbayev. He controls major stakes in Kazakhstan's energy, banking, and infrastructure sectors through Samruk-Kazyna, the national wealth fund, and private holdings, making him one of the most powerful figures in Central Asian business.
profiles.connectionNote: The marriage of Soviet-era elite and post-Soviet business power, Kulibayev and his wife Dinara represent the Russian-speaking Kazakhstani oligarchy that emerged from the USSR's collapse. All his business dealings are conducted in Russian — the language of post-Soviet Central Asian business — and he is deeply embedded in the Russian-speaking business world that spans Kazakhstan, Russia, and beyond.
achievements: Controls major stakes in Kazakhstan's energy, banking, and infrastructure
Husband of Dinara Nazarbayeva — daughter of founding President Nazarbayev
Former chairman of Samruk-Kazyna — Kazakhstan's national wealth fund
Major shareholder in KazMunayGas and Kazkommertsbank
One of the most powerful figures in Central Asian business

profiles.name: Dinara Kulibayeva
profiles.tagline: Daughter of Kazakhstan's founding president — wealthiest woman in Central Asia
profiles.bio: Dinara Kulibayeva is a Kazakhstani businesswoman and the daughter of founding President Nursultan Nazarbayev. Through her husband Timur Kulibayev and her own holdings — including a major stake in Halyk Bank, Kazakhstan's largest private bank — she is consistently listed as the wealthiest woman in Central Asia.
profiles.connectionNote: Born into the family that built the Kazakhstani state, Dinara Kulibayeva embodies the intersection of political power and private wealth in the post-Soviet Central Asian context. Her Russian-language business world and her position at the top of Kazakhstani society make her a defining figure of the Russian-speaking post-Soviet elite.
achievements: Major shareholder in Halyk Bank — Kazakhstan's largest private bank
Wealthiest woman in Central Asia (Forbes)
Daughter of founding President Nursultan Nazarbayev

Net worth estimated at $4+ billion

profiles.name: Nurlan Smagulov
profiles.tagline: Kazakhstan's automotive and real estate billionaire — newest entrant on Forbes global list
profiles.bio: Nurlan Smagulov is a Kazakhstani billionaire who built his fortune in the automotive distribution and real estate sectors in Central Asia. He is one of the newer entrants on the Forbes global billionaires list, controlling major automotive dealership networks and property assets across Kazakhstan.
profiles.connectionNote: A product of the post-Soviet Kazakhstani business environment where Russian is the dominant business language, Smagulov operates entirely within the Russian-speaking economic sphere of Central Asia. His rise reflects Kazakhstan's emergence as a significant economic power in the post-Soviet space.
achievements: Forbes global billionaires list — automotive and real estate
Major automotive distribution network across Kazakhstan
Significant real estate holdings in Almaty and Nur-Sultan

profiles.name: Aydin Rakhimbayev
profiles.tagline: Kazakhstani construction billionaire who built BI Group into Central Asia's largest development company
profiles.bio: Aydin Rakhimbayev is a Kazakhstani billionaire and founder of BI Group — one of Central Asia's largest construction and real estate development companies. He is one of the newer entrants on the Forbes global billionaires list and a prominent figure in Kazakhstan's booming construction sector.
profiles.connectionNote: Building one of Central Asia's largest companies in a Russian-speaking business environment, Rakhimbayev operates within the post-Soviet Kazakhstani economy where Russian remains the dominant language of commerce and business. His BI Group has shaped the physical landscape of modern Kazakhstan.
achievements: Founder of BI Group — Central Asia's largest construction company
Forbes global billionaires list
BI Group built thousands of residential and commercial properties across Kazakhstan
Major figure in Kazakhstan's construction and real estate boom
One of Central Asia's most prominent business leaders

profiles.name: Bidzina Ivanishvili
profiles.tagline: Made his fortune in 1990s Moscow metals and banking — became Georgia's Prime Minister and richest man
profiles.bio: Bidzina Ivanishvili is a Georgian billionaire and politician who made his fortune in Moscow during the chaotic 1990s in metals and banking before returning to Georgia, founding the Georgian Dream party, and serving as Prime Minister (2012-2013). He remains the dominant political and economic force in Georgia and is the country's wealthiest individual.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in a small Georgian village, Ivanishvili built his multi-billion dollar fortune entirely in Moscow — in the same post-Soviet privatisation chaos that produced Russia's oligarchs. He speaks fluent Russian, his business world was Russian, and his return to Georgian politics as an outsider backed by Moscow-made wealth has defined Georgian political life for over a decade.
achievements: Made multi-billion dollar fortune in Moscow metals and banking (1990s)
Prime Minister of Georgia (2012-2013) — Georgian Dream party founder
Georgia's wealthiest individual — net worth $5+ billion
Owns Château La Citadelle winery in Bordeaux
Dominant figure in Georgian politics despite formal retirement from office

profiles.name: Vagit Alekperov
profiles.tagline: Born in Baku — founded LUKOIL, Russia's largest private oil company, and became one of the world's richest men
profiles.bio: Vagit Alekperov is an Azerbaijani-Russian billionaire born in Baku who founded LUKOIL — Russia's largest privately-owned oil company and one of the world's biggest energy producers. Rising through the Soviet oil industry from Baku to Moscow, he built LUKOIL into a global energy giant with operations in over 30 countries.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Baku (Azerbaijani SSR) in 1950 to an Azerbaijani oil worker father, Alekperov rose through the Soviet oil industry — working in Baku, Western Siberia, and Moscow — before founding LUKOIL in 1991. His Azerbaijani origins and his Russian business career perfectly embody the pan-Soviet Russian-speaking business world this expanded section charts.
achievements: Founded LUKOIL (1991) — Russia's largest privately-owned oil company
LUKOIL operates in 30+ countries globally
Net worth peaked at $25+ billion (Forbes)
Deputy Minister of Fuel and Energy of the USSR before founding LUKOIL
Resigned from LUKOIL board following Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine

profiles.name: Ruben Vardanyan
profiles.tagline: Founded Troika Dialog — Russia's greatest investment bank — then renounced Russian citizenship and was imprisoned in Azerbaijan
profiles.bio: Ruben Vardanyan is an Armenian-Russian investment banker and philanthropist who founded Troika Dialog — once Russia's largest and most prestigious investment bank — and built a career at the centre of Russian finance for three decades. He later renounced his Russian citizenship, moved to Nagorno-Karabakh as its State Minister, and was imprisoned by Azerbaijan following the 2023 fall of Karabakh.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Yerevan (Armenian SSR) in 1968 and building his career at Moscow State University before founding Troika Dialog in 1991, Vardanyan spent decades as one of the defining figures of Russian high finance — his bank at the centre of Russia's post-Soviet transformation. His later renunciation of Russian citizenship and imprisonment in Azerbaijan make him one of the most dramatic post-Soviet figures in this database.
achievements: Founded Troika Dialog (1991) — Russia's most prestigious investment bank for two decades
Sold Troika Dialog to Sberbank for approximately $1 billion (2012)
Renounced Russian citizenship and moved to Nagorno-Karabakh as State Minister (2022)
Imprisoned by Azerbaijan following fall of Karabakh (2023)
Co-founded UWC Dilijan College in Armenia — world-class international school
profiles.quote: I chose Armenia over Russia. I have no regrets.

profiles.name: Vladimir Potanin
profiles.tagline: Born in Moscow — controls Norilsk Nickel, the world's largest producer of nickel and palladium
profiles.bio: Vladimir Potanin is a Russian billionaire who controls Norilsk Nickel — the world's largest producer of nickel and palladium and a major copper and platinum producer. One of the architects of Russia's 1990s privatisation era, he obtained Norilsk Nickel through the infamous 'loans-for-shares' scheme and built it into a global mining giant. Consistently Russia's richest or second-richest individual.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Moscow in 1961 into a family of Soviet trade officials, Potanin rose through the Soviet foreign trade apparatus before becoming one of the key figures of Russia's post-Soviet privatisation. His acquisition of Norilsk Nickel — through a scheme he co-designed — became the defining example of how Soviet-era insiders captured Russia's greatest industrial assets.
achievements: Controls Norilsk Nickel — world's largest nickel and palladium producer
One of the architects of Russia's loans-for-shares privatisation scheme (1995)
Consistently Russia's richest or second-richest individual (Forbes)
Net worth $30+ billion at peak
Announced Norilsk Nickel would not leave Russia after 2022 invasion

profiles.name: Alexei Mordashov
profiles.tagline: Born in Cherepovets — controls Severstal, Russia's most efficient steelmaker, and was the majority shareholder of TUI travel group
profiles.bio: Alexei Mordashov is a Russian billionaire who built Severstal — one of the world's most efficient integrated steel companies — and was for years the largest shareholder of TUI Group, one of the world's biggest travel and tourism companies. Currently Russia's wealthiest individual on many lists with a fortune of $25+ billion.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Cherepovets in 1965 — a Vologda Oblast steel city — Mordashov worked his way up from the Cherepovets metallurgical plant's finance department to full ownership. His TUI shareholding — connecting Russian steel money to European mass tourism — was one of the most tangible examples of Russian oligarch capital embedded in European consumer industry before 2022.
achievements: Controls Severstal — Russia's most profitable integrated steel company
Was largest shareholder of TUI Group — world's biggest travel company
Net worth $25+ billion — frequently Russia's richest individual
Sanctioned by EU and UK following Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine
Exited TUI shareholding after sanctions

profiles.name: Tatyana Bakalchuk
profiles.tagline: Founded Wildberries from her Moscow apartment while on maternity leave — built the largest e-commerce platform in Russia
profiles.bio: Tatyana Bakalchuk is a Russian entrepreneur who founded Wildberries — Russia's largest online marketplace — from her Moscow apartment in 2004 while on maternity leave. She built it into a company processing billions of orders annually with operations across Russia, the CIS, and beyond, making her the richest woman in the Russian-speaking world and one of Russia's wealthiest individuals.
profiles.connectionNote: A former English teacher who started her business with a small investment and no outside funding, Bakalchuk's story of building Wildberries into Russia's Amazon from her apartment while raising children is one of the most remarkable entrepreneurial narratives in post-Soviet history. She is the only self-made female billionaire currently active in Russia.
achievements: Founded Wildberries (2004) from her Moscow apartment — now Russia's largest e-commerce platform
Richest woman in the Russian-speaking world (Forbes)
Wildberries processes hundreds of millions of orders annually
Expanded Wildberries to CIS countries and beyond
Self-funded from the start — no outside investors
profiles.quote: I started with almost nothing, while on maternity leave. The idea was simple: make shopping easy.

profiles.name: Evgeny Lebedev
profiles.tagline: Son of a KGB officer — owns the Evening Standard and The Independent, sits in the House of Lords
profiles.bio: Evgeny Lebedev is a Russian-British media proprietor and member of the House of Lords (Baron Lebedev of Hampton and Siberia) who owns the Evening Standard and The Independent. The son of Alexander Lebedev — a former KGB officer turned oligarch — he was ennobled by Prime Minister Boris Johnson in 2020, generating significant controversy.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Moscow in 1980 to Alexander Lebedev — a KGB officer who later became a banker and newspaper owner — Evgeny came to Britain and built his media empire as a cultural patron and newspaper proprietor. His ennoblement by Boris Johnson, despite security concerns raised by officials, became one of the most controversial peerage appointments in modern British history.
achievements: Owner of the Evening Standard and The Independent
Baron Lebedev of Hampton and Siberia — House of Lords (2020)
Ennobled by PM Boris Johnson — appointment generated major controversy
Son of Alexander Lebedev — former KGB officer and Russian oligarch
Art collector and cultural patron in London

profiles.name: Andrey Andreev
profiles.tagline: Russian-born founder of Badoo who launched Bumble — the dating app that made women message first
profiles.bio: Andrey Andreev is a Russian-born British entrepreneur who founded Badoo — one of the world's largest dating platforms — and was the original investor and infrastructure provider behind Bumble, the dating app that made women message first. He sold his stake in Bumble's parent company MagicLab in 2019.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Russia and building his business in London, Andreev created two of the world's most significant dating platforms. Badoo — which he built from scratch — has over 500 million registered users. His seed funding and infrastructure for Bumble, founded by Whitney Wolfe Herd, created a company that went public at a $13 billion valuation.
achievements: Founded Badoo — 500+ million registered users worldwide
Original investor and backer of Bumble — dating app that IPO'd at $13B valuation
Sold MagicLab (Badoo/Bumble) to Blackstone for $3 billion (2019)
One of the most successful Russian-born internet entrepreneurs in the UK
Built London into a hub for Russian tech entrepreneurship

profiles.name: Igor Bukhman
profiles.tagline: Russian-born co-founder of Playrix — one of the world's largest mobile gaming companies
profiles.bio: Igor Bukhman is a Russian-born Irish-based entrepreneur who co-founded Playrix with his brother Dmitry — one of the world's largest mobile gaming companies. Their games Homescapes, Gardenscapes, and Township have been downloaded billions of times globally. Playrix is based in Ireland and reportedly valued at $7+ billion.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Russia and building Playrix from modest beginnings with his brother Dmitry, Igor Bukhman represents the generation of Russian software developers who built globally dominant consumer technology companies. The Bukhman brothers' story — from Russian coders to running one of the world's biggest mobile gaming empires from Ireland — is one of the most successful Russian tech diaspora stories.

Homescapes, Gardenscapes, Township — billions of downloads combined
Playrix reportedly valued at $7+ billion
Based in Ireland — major employer in European tech
Among the most successful Russian gaming entrepreneurs globally

profiles.name: Dmitry Bukhman
profiles.tagline: Co-founded Playrix with brother Igor — together they built one of the world's biggest mobile gaming empires
profiles.bio: Dmitry Bukhman is a Russian-born entrepreneur who co-founded Playrix with his brother Igor — one of the world's largest mobile gaming companies with billions of downloads. He co-manages the company's global operations from Ireland alongside his brother.
profiles.connectionNote: The Bukhman brothers — Igor and Dmitry — built Playrix together from Russia into a global mobile gaming powerhouse. Their story is one of the most successful examples of Russian programming talent creating a globally dominant consumer product. Their collaboration represents the best of the Russian technical education tradition applied to consumer software.
achievements: Co-founded Playrix with brother Igor — billions of game downloads worldwide
Homescapes, Gardenscapes, Township — dominant mobile puzzle games
Playrix valued at $7+ billion

profiles.name: Victor Pinchuk
profiles.parents: Father-in-law: Leonid Kuchma (former President of Ukraine)
profiles.tagline: Ukrainian steel magnate and philanthropist — his Yalta European Strategy forum defined Ukraine's path to Europe
profiles.bio: Victor Pinchuk is a Ukrainian billionaire steel magnate and philanthropist who controls EastOne Group and built Ukraine's largest steel pipe company Interpipe. He is also the founder of the Yalta European Strategy (YES) forum — one of Europe's most important foreign policy conferences — and the PinchukArtCentre, Ukraine's leading contemporary art museum.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Kyiv in 1960, Pinchuk made his fortune in steel pipes before becoming Ukraine's most internationally connected philanthropist and businessman. His YES forum — bringing European and global leaders to Yalta annually before Russia's 2014 occupation of Crimea — was one of the most consequential vehicles for Ukraine's European integration. His father-in-law is former President Leonid Kuchma.
achievements: Built Interpipe — Ukraine's largest steel pipe manufacturer
Founded Yalta European Strategy (YES) — one of Europe's key foreign policy forums
Founded PinchukArtCentre — Ukraine's leading contemporary art institution
Major philanthropist — Elton John AIDS Foundation, Clinton Global Initiative
Father-in-law: Leonid Kuchma — former President of Ukraine

profiles.name: Kostyantyn Zhevago
profiles.tagline: Ukrainian iron ore billionaire who took Ferrexpo to the London Stock Exchange
profiles.bio: Kostyantyn Zhevago is a Ukrainian billionaire who controls Ferrexpo — a major iron ore pellet producer listed on the London Stock Exchange — and Finance & Credit Bank (now defunct). He was one of Ukraine's richest businessmen and a long-serving Member of the Verkhovna Rada.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Poltava region and building his business empire in the chaos of post-Soviet Ukrainian privatisation, Zhevago took Ferrexpo public in London — making him one of the very few Ukrainian businessmen to list a major company on the LSE. He has faced legal proceedings in multiple jurisdictions in recent years.
achievements: Controls Ferrexpo — iron ore pellet producer listed on London Stock Exchange
Former Member of the Verkhovna Rada — Ukrainian parliament
One of Ukraine's wealthiest individuals in the 2000s-2010s
Built business empire during post-Soviet Ukrainian privatisation
Ferrexpo supplies iron ore to European steelmakers

profiles.name: Harry Triguboff
profiles.parents: Parents: Russian-Jewish refugees who fled to China
profiles.tagline: 'High-Rise Harry' — born in China to Russian-Jewish refugees, built Australia's largest apartment empire
profiles.bio: Harry Triguboff is an Australian billionaire born in Dalian, China to Russian-Jewish parents who had fled Russia. He founded Meriton — Australia's largest private residential developer, responsible for building more apartments than any other company in Australian history — and is consistently one of the wealthiest Australians.
profiles.connectionNote: His parents were Russian-Jewish refugees who fled Russia and settled in Harbin and Dalian, China — part of the large Russian-Jewish émigré community in Manchuria — before emigrating to Australia. Triguboff grew up speaking Russian and maintains his connection to the language. His journey from Russian-Jewish refugee child in China to building Australia's skyline is one of the great diaspora success stories.
achievements: Founded Meriton — Australia's largest private residential developer
Built more apartments than any other company in Australian history
Consistently one of the two or three wealthiest Australians (Forbes)
Net worth $10+ billion
Born to Russian-Jewish refugee parents in Dalian, China — speaks Russian

profiles.name: Bulat Utemuratov
profiles.tagline: Former aide to Nazarbayev who built Verny Capital and became one of Kazakhstan's key financial figures
profiles.bio: Bulat Utemuratov is a Kazakhstani billionaire who served as an aide to President Nursultan Nazarbayev before building Verny Capital — a diversified investment group spanning banking, telecoms, hospitality, and real estate including a Burger King franchise across Central Asia. He is one of Kazakhstan's most important private sector figures.
profiles.connectionNote: A product of the Kazakhstani political and business elite formed under Nazarbayev, Utemuratov represents the generation of post-Soviet Central Asian businessmen who converted political access into commercial empires. All his business dealings are conducted in Russian — the language of Kazakhstani business — and his investments span the Russian-speaking world.

Former aide to President Nursultan Nazarbayev
Burger King franchise operator across Central Asia

profiles.name: Alexander Tamas
profiles.tagline: Post-Soviet-born Silicon Valley tech investor — backed SpaceX and co-founded Vy Capital
profiles.bio: Alexander Tamas is a post-Soviet-born technology investor and co-founder of Vy Capital — a major Silicon Valley investment firm that was among the early backers of SpaceX, Twitter, and other leading technology companies. He is one of the most connected Russian-speaking investors in the global technology ecosystem.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in the post-Soviet space and building his investment career in Silicon Valley, Tamas represents the generation of Russian-speaking immigrants who became significant forces in American venture capital. His Vy Capital co-investments alongside leading US funds placed Russian-speaking capital at the heart of the world's most valuable technology companies.

Early backer of SpaceX
Investment portfolio spanning leading US technology companies

profiles.name: Nikita Kahn
profiles.tagline: Russian-born philanthropist and real estate developer — partner of Oracle founder Larry Ellison
profiles.bio: Nikita Kahn is a Russian-born American philanthropist, model, and real estate developer who is the long-term partner of Larry Ellison — the Oracle founder and one of the world's wealthiest individuals. She is actively involved in philanthropic work including animal welfare and has worked on real estate development projects.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Russia/Ukraine and having built a life in the United States, Kahn represents the generation of Russian-speaking women who emigrated and achieved prominence in American society. Her partnership with Larry Ellison — and her philanthropic work — place her in the broader Russian-speaking American elite.

Philanthropist — animal welfare and other causes
Real estate development projects in California
Russian-born — fluent Russian speaker in American public life

profiles.name: Vaja Jhashi
profiles.tagline: Georgian-born agricultural billionaire who built Trans-Oil into the Black Sea grain trading giant
profiles.bio: Vaja Jhashi is a Georgian-born Moldovan billionaire who founded Trans-Oil — one of the largest agribusiness and grain trading companies in the Black Sea region, operating across Moldova, Ukraine, and Eastern Europe. He is the wealthiest person in Moldova.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in Soviet Georgia and building his business empire in Moldova — a former Soviet republic where Russian remains widely spoken — Jhashi operates entirely within the Russian-speaking post-Soviet business world. Trans-Oil is one of the most significant agribusiness companies in the Black Sea grain export corridor.
achievements: Founded Trans-Oil — one of the largest agribusiness companies in the Black Sea region
Wealthiest person in Moldova (Forbes)
Operations spanning Moldova, Ukraine, and Eastern Europe
Major player in Black Sea grain export trade

profiles.name: Artem Milinchuk
profiles.tagline: Post-Soviet-born founder of FarmTogether — pioneering digital farmland investment for ultra-high-net-worth individuals
profiles.bio: Artem Milinchuk is a post-Soviet-born American entrepreneur who founded FarmTogether — a digital investment platform that allows institutional and high-net-worth investors to invest in US farmland. He pioneered the alternative asset class of digital farmland investing and has become a prominent voice in the UHNW investment community.
profiles.connectionNote: Born in the post-Soviet space and building his career in the United States, Milinchuk represents the generation of Russian-speaking entrepreneurs who found their niche in American alternative finance. FarmTogether's model — making farmland accessible as a diversified investment — attracted significant investor interest from the UHNW community globally.
achievements: Founded FarmTogether — leading digital farmland investment platform
Pioneered farmland as an accessible alternative asset class for UHNW investors
Raised significant venture capital for FarmTogether
Prominent voice in alternative investment and UHNW finance communities
